46. DISPOSITION OF THE MOON.
If the Moon is conjunct benefics she will always prove beneficial.
If the Moon joins Mars/Saturn, she is always inauspicious; this does not apply to her joining
Mars/Saturn in the 10th so, that the native becomes an Army chief.
47-50. MARS-MERCURY CONJUNCTION IN ANGLES.
One, who has Mars and Mercury in conjunction in the Ascendant will be murderous (or cruel, or
mischievous), be skillful in dealing with jobs related to fire, be a metallurgist/mineralogist, be
industrious, be an ambassador (or a messenger) and will enjoy concealed/secret properties.
If Mars and Mercury are conjunct in the 4th, one will be devoid of relatives, but endowed with
friends, wealth, food, pleasures and conveyances and will be discarded by his own people.
If Mars and Mercury are conjunct in the 7th, one will wander from country to country (aimlessly),
will serve base people, be skillful in arguments and will lose his first wife.
